title: sizeof should not be called on pointers
description: sizeof returns the size in bytes of a type. One common usage pattern, especially in C, is to use sizeof to determine the size of an array. However, arrays decay to pointers when passed as arguments to a function, and if sizeof is applied to such an argument, it will return the size of the pointer, not of the array.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Sizeof should not be called on pointers. Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
